checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=29596106.52 (2014-12-31), implying DSO ~113 days vs Q4 2013 DSO ~83 days
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=46996771.27 (2015-06-30) vs SalesRevenueGoodsNet=28842414.58 (2015-04-01 to 2015-06-30), implying DSO ~147 days
SalesRevenueGoodsNet grew from 29597324.11 (FY2013) to 72893821.08 (FY2014), ~2.5x, while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 9082048.73 (2013-12-31) to 29596106.52 (2014-12-31), ~3.3x

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: OtherAssetsNoncurrent=224037.67 (2010-12-31)
OtherAssetsNoncurrent=115671.62 (2015-06-30), roughly flat/declining despite revenue growth

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic=50698263.18 (2013-01-01 to 2013-12-31) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=-16156281.62 (2013-01-01 to 2013-12-31)
evidence.location: 10-K FY2013 income statement and cash flow statement

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic=60643587.49 (2013-10-01 to 2013-12-31), a single-quarter spike driving the full-year result
StockholdersEquity=38663544.10 (2013-09-30) rising to StockholdersEquity=98563180.42 (2013-12-31), a ~$60M one-quarter increase matching the net income spike

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=2348742.73 (2013-12-31) growing to InventoryNet=13301018.95 (2014-12-31), ~5.7x increase
SalesRevenueGoodsNet=29597324.11 (FY2013) growing to SalesRevenueGoodsNet=72893821.08 (FY2014), only ~2.5x increase

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic=-13472699.99 (2012-01-01 to 2012-12-31)
NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic=50698263.18 (2013-01-01 to 2013-12-31)
NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic=-2178279.28 (2014-01-01 to 2014-12-31)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=1582874.83 (2015-03-31) falling to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=317792.56 (2015-06-30), an ~80% decline
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 38989859.83 (2015-03-31) to 46996771.27 (2015-06-30) over the same period the allowance fell

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: NT 10-K filing_date=2014-03-17 preceding 10-K filing_date=2014-03-31
NT 10-K filing_date=2015-03-17 preceding 10-K filing_date=2015-03-20
8-K/A filing_date=2014-03-11 (amended current report)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data provided from CashAndCashEquivalentsAtCarryingValue end=2008-12-31 through AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end=2015-06-30 across Assets, Revenues, AR, Inventory, Cash Flow, and Equity concepts

Revenue concept tags shift over time: SalesRevenueNet (2009-2012), SalesRevenueServicesNet, then SalesRevenueGoodsNet (2012-2015), reflecting an apparent business model change that somewhat limits clean like-for-like comparisons

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature/aggressive revenue recognition (e.g., extended payment terms, bill-and-hold, or channel stuffing) combined with under-reserving for doubtful accounts, inflating net receivables and revenue while understating the allowance needed to reflect collectibility risk.

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent fell from 1582874.83 (2015-03-31) to 317792.56 (2015-06-30) while AR grew from 38989859.83 to 46996771.27 over the same quarter

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: A large, likely non-cash, non-operating gain (e.g., fair value remeasurement of warrants/derivatives, bargain purchase gain, or gain on debt/equity restructuring) recognized in Q4 2013 drove reported net income and equity sharply higher without corresponding operating cash generation, potentially overstating sustainable earnings quality.

Inventory build-up substantially outpacing revenue growth (possible failure to write down slow-moving/obsolete inventory, or recording of goods shipped to distributors/channel as sold while remaining unsold), which would overstate current assets and potentially understate cost of goods sold/future write-off risk.

top_signals: Rapidly rising days-sales-outstanding (DSO climbing from ~83 to ~147 days over 2013-2015) with AccountsReceivableNetCurrent growing faster than revenue
Sharp ~80% quarter-over-quarter decline in AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent (1,582,874.83 to 317,792.56) even as receivables continued to grow
Large disconnect between FY2013 net income (+50,698,263.18) and operating cash flow (-16,156,281.62), driven by an unexplained $60.6M Q4 2013 net income spike and matching equity jump
Inventory growing ~5.7x versus revenue growth of ~2.5x from 2013 to 2014
Two consecutive years of NT 10-K late-filing notices (2014 and 2015) signaling recurring reporting/closing difficulties
